commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=ee1f5ac29012186ab9519e72e39f3a770930211c)
commands.review_summary:
The PR removes CLI exclusions from memory-flush gating, adds backend-managed context hiding to the usage footer contract/template, and imports a message_tool_only cascade guard with tests.

PR surface: Source +18, Tests +148. Total +166 across 12 files.

Reproducibility: Do we have a high-confidence way to reproduce the issue? Yes at source level: current main skips CLI memoryFlush, documents that CLI backends skip it, and message_tool_only delivery continues unless terminate is returned. I did not run a live CLI or Telegram reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Manual review is needed because the PR changes native CLI session semantics and message-delivery invariants, and contributor-side real behavior proof is still missing.

target.title: fix(cli): enable pre-compaction memory flush for CLI backends and hide fake context bar (#96858)
target.body:
## Summary

CLI backends (claude-cli, codex-cli) declare `ownsNativeCompaction: true`, meaning OpenClaw defers compaction to the native harness. However, memory flush (handoff/dreaming file writes) is **orthogonal** to compaction — it provides a recovery point before the native compaction runs.

Two `!isCli` gates in `runMemoryFlushIfNeeded` incorrectly blocked memory flush for all CLI sessions, meaning any failure during native compaction would lose the pre-compaction state entirely.

Separately, the usage bar unconditionally rendered a context window meter for CLI sessions, showing a meaningless 100% value since OpenClaw doesn't manage the CLI's context window.

## Root cause evidence

### Part A — Memory flush blocked for CLI

`src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-memory.ts` contains **three** CLI gates. Only one is correct:

| Line | Gate | Intent | Correct? |
|------|------|--------|----------|
| 758 | `isCli` early return | Preflight compaction skip — CLI owns compaction | Kept |
| 1097 | `!isCli` in `canAttemptFlush` | Memory flush attempt — mistakenly skips CLI | Removed |
| 1250-1253 | `!isCli` in `shouldFlushMemory` | Second memory flush gate — same mistake | Removed |

Proof of orthogonality:
- `extensions/anthropic/cli-backend.ts:38` — `ownsNativeCompaction: true` only, nothing about memory flush
- `src/agents/command/cli-compaction.ts:561` — checks `resolvedBackend?.ownsNativeCompaction` only, independent path
- Memory flush writes `handoffFile` / `dreamingFile` (recovery points), never touches compaction

### Part B — Usage bar lacks backend awareness

`context.pct_used` in `src/auto-reply/usage-bar/contract.ts:39-40` is calculated from `state.contextUsedTokens / state.contextTokenBudget`. For CLI sessions, OpenClaw cannot observe the true context occupancy, leading to an alarming but meaningless 100% display.

The template in `src/auto-reply/usage-bar/default-template.ts:34-35` unconditionally renders the context bar — no backend type check existed.

## Changes

### Part A — Memory flush (2 lines removed)

- `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-memory.ts:1097` — removed `!isCli` from `canAttemptFlush`
- `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-memory.ts:1250-1253` — removed `!isCli` from `shouldFlushMemory`

Safety: after removal, memory flush is still guarded by `memoryFlushWritable`, `!isHeartbeat`, and `shouldRunMemoryFlush()` threshold logic.

### Part B — Usage bar context awareness (7 files, additive)

- `src/plugins/hook-types.ts` — added `isCliBackend` field to `PluginHookReplyUsageState`
- `src/auto-reply/reply/reply-usage-state.ts` — parameter to propagation
- `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.ts` — `isCliBackend: isCliProvider(providerUsed, cfg)`
- `src/auto-reply/reply/followup-runner.ts` — `isCliBackend: usedCliProvider`
- `src/auto-reply/usage-bar/contract.ts` — `context.managed_by_backend` field
- `src/auto-reply/usage-bar/default-template.ts` — `unless` on context bar segment
- `src/auto-reply/usage-bar/translator.ts` — added `unless` support

## Test results

### agent-runner-memory.test.ts (47 tests, all pass)

New tests confirming CLI sessions now execute memory flush:
- `runs memory flush for CLI providers (regression: #96858)`
- `runs memory flush for compatible CLI session runtime pins (regression: #96858)`
- `runs memory flush for CLI backends (regression: #96858)`

Two existing tests updated from "expect skip" to "expect flush" (previously they tested the buggy behavior).

### translator.test.ts (16 tests, all pass)

New tests confirming context bar hiding:
- `unless drops when the field is truthy, keeps when falsy or missing`
- `hides the context bar when managed_by_backend is true`
- `renders the context bar when managed_by_backend is false`

### TypeScript & Lint

- `npx tsc --noEmit` — no errors in changed files
- `npx oxlint` — clean on all 10 changed files
